audit_items.proof_trace: The three raw-pointer functions in string_utils.h that lack null guards (hash_string, string_as_array, resize_uninitialized) are dead code — Grep confirms zero callers anywhere in the codebase. The only live callers of c_escape_string/xml_escape_string/json_escape_string use const std::string& overloads or pass fixed-size stack arrays, neither of which can be NULL. No external RPC path reaches any of the vulnerable raw-pointer functions.
